question: what is ETT
content:
The simplest rule that fixes the most mistakes.
E.T.T means that your entire body and the ball must always point towards your target and your breakpoint, not just your eyes.
If some parts of your body point somewhere else, your ball will follow that direction instead.
When E.T.T is right, your swing becomes straighter, your timing becomes cleaner, and your accuracy improves immediately.
1. BODY ALIGNMENT (THE FOUNDATION)
Before you even move your feet, your shoulders, hips, feet, and the ball must all face the same line:
From your start position
Through your on lane target
All the way to your breakpoint
2. FEET & WALK, E.T.T WHILE MOVING
Your walk SHOULD follow the same direction as your body alignment.
Example:
If you’re aiming at 2nd arrow, with breakpoint at 6 board, or even deeper to that same breakpoint, make sure that your start position matches an invisible line from your feet through your target till your breakpoint. Then, your walk goes in a smooth, controlled line that keeps the ball on track to reach that breakpoint naturally.
3. PUSH AWAY & SWING, THE MOST IMPORTANT PART OF E.T.T
After the push away, the ball should travel on a straight line toward your target.
The swing should move naturally forward → down → back → forward on THAT same line.
What E.T.T prevents:
the ball going behind your back
rounded, inconsistent swing
golf swing motion
over rotation
missed target
loss of accuracy
Example
If your push away points directly to your target, gravity will keep your swing on that same track.
But if you push away to the side, the swing goes off the line and forces:
missed arrows
missed breakpoint
inconsistent release
4. BREAKPOINT ALIGNMENT (THE REAL TARGET)
The breakpoint is the most important point your ball needs to reach.
E.T.T ensures your entire setup, and the free swing sends the ball exactly there.
Example
If you bowling deep inside and your breakpoint is at 8 board, using E.T.T makes sure that:  your start position, your feet, hips, shoulders, push away, swing path…all point toward that exact location.

5. THE SIMPLE E.T.T RULE YOU CAN TEACH ANYONE
If your feet, body, swing, and ball all point to the same line,
your ball will follow that line every single time.
